message:
feat(canvas): CanvasPixelRect + render_excluding, plus visual-effects showcase wiring

Adds the missing primitive for "render the canvas everywhere
EXCEPT this rectangle". The visual-effects showcase has a
foreground panel (the demo's chrome — title, controls, frame
counter overlay) that previously had to be redrawn ON TOP of the
finished canvas every frame. That works visually but pays the
cost of the canvas painter for cells the foreground panel
immediately overwrites — wasted work in the showcase's tightest
hot path, where the same painter runs ~60 times per second
against a >100k-pixel canvas.

== crates/ftui-extras/src/canvas.rs ==

Three new public items:

  - `CanvasPixelRect`: a value-type rectangle in painter sub-pixel
    coordinates (the unit the canvas painter actually consumes,
    which differs from cell coordinates by `Mode::cols_per_cell()`
    × `Mode::rows_per_cell()`). Stores `x`, `y`, `width`, `height`
    as `usize` since sub-pixel grids can be larger than `u16`.
  - `CanvasPixelRect::from_cell_intersection(cell_rect,
    canvas_area, mode)`: converts an absolute terminal-cell `Rect`
    into the matching sub-pixel rectangle, clipped to the canvas
    area. Returns `None` when the input rectangle has no
    intersection with the canvas (so the caller's exclude
    short-circuits to "nothing to exclude" naturally). The cell-to-
    pixel conversion uses `usize::from(local_x) * cols` rather than
    `local_x as usize * cols` to make the unit conversion grep-
    visible.
  - `CanvasPixelRect::is_empty(self)`: const helper for the common
    "exclude is degenerate" check at call sites.

Plus a new instance method on the painter:

  - `render_excluding(&self, area, frame, exclude)`: identical to
    the existing `render(area, frame)` except it computes the
    pixel-rect for `exclude` via `CanvasPixelRect::from_cell_intersection`
    and skips painting any pixel whose owning cell falls inside
    that rect. The exclusion check is a single bounds comparison
    per pixel; degenerate or empty excludes short-circuit before
    the loop runs.

== crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/canvas_adapters.rs ==

The plasma + scrim + backdrop adapters that wrap painters from
`visual_fx` into canvas-renderable shapes get a parallel
`render_excluding` method on each adapter. The exclude argument
flows through unchanged to the underlying painter's
`render_excluding`. No new shape rules; this is pure plumbing so
showcase code can wire the exclude through without unwrapping the
adapter types.

== crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs ==

Wires the new method into the visual-effects demo's render path.
The foreground chrome rect (computed via the existing layout
code) is passed as the exclude region to the canvas painter, so
the painter never produces output for cells the chrome will
immediately overwrite. The chrome itself is rendered last, on
top of the (now smaller) canvas output.

Also adds a `RenderStrategy` toggle in the demo's control panel
so the user can flip between `RenderAll` (legacy behavior, paints
under the chrome) and `RenderExcluding` (new behavior). Visual
output is identical in both modes — the chrome covers the
under-painted region in either case — but the demo's
"frames per second" indicator visibly updates when the toggle
flips, since the painter is doing strictly less work in
`RenderExcluding` mode.

Useful as a regression guard: future canvas painter work that
breaks the exclude path will show up as either a visual artifact
(painter output bleeding through the chrome region) or as a
painter time regression in the demo's FPS readout.
